title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
summary: Aluminum prices fell as traders reacted to reports that President Donald Trump may narrow U.S. import tariffs on some aluminum and steel products. The clip says LME aluminum dropped as much as 2.7% and was still trading lower as broader metals markets sold off.

title: America's big new aluminum smelter is still waiting on a power deal
summary: The energy-hungry facility will be America's first new smelter since 1980 and is expected to more than double the nation's capacity for making primary aluminum. However, the project remains stalled as developers await a finalized power agreement to proceed.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has announced a decision to boost its production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariff policies. Fox News senior correspondent Steve Harrigan reports this marks a significant increase in output for the major US producer.

title: NEW: Trump's tariffs credited for this MAJOR boost to US aluminum production
summary: Fox News senior correspondent Steve Harrigan details how President Trump's tariffs are credited with driving Century Aluminum's decision to significantly boost production. The report highlights a major shift in US aluminum output capacity linked directly to recent trade policies.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminum industry decarbonize
summary: This article highlights Hydro's HalZero technology, which eliminates CO2 emissions during aluminum smelting by keeping carbon and chlorine in a closed loop. It also covers innovations in removing metal impurities from recycled aluminum and Rio Tinto's use of hydrogen to replace natural gas in alumina refining, all advancing green aluminum sustainability.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process that captures over 99% of aluminum ions from hazardous waste streams in aluminum production. The captured aluminum is upcycled into bulk production, increasing yield while significantly reducing waste and environmental impact.

title: Current Challenges and Opportunities for the Aluminum Transformation Industry in the United States
summary: The report identifies the two main challenges for the aluminum industry: reducing greenhouse gas emissions and increasing recycling rates. It explores strategic opportunities to address these issues through technological innovation and policy support.

title: New & Innovative Markets
summary: A snapshot report shows that the U.S. aluminum industry has achieved positive environmental impact trends across nearly all measures over the past 25 years. The industry continues to innovate in new markets while improving sustainability.

title: Aluminum: Driving Efficiency and Sustainability Across All Vehicle Segments
summary: Recycling aluminum uses approximately 95% less energy than primary production, resulting in 95% fewer carbon emissions. This efficiency makes aluminum a critical material for sustainable vehicle manufacturing across all segments.

title: US steel demand 'nowhere near' prior expectations
summary: This video discussion says US steel demand remains weaker than earlier expectations, with construction described as down and autos holding up more steadily. It also says MEPS assessed flat steel product prices lower and expects prices to end the year below current levels.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has notified PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine, to drastically cut its ore production quota from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons this year to boost global nickel prices. The company intends to apply for a revision of this quota, which impacts supply for stainless steel and electric-vehicle batteries.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia's Minister of Energy and Mineral Resources confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue while cracking down on environmentally harmful operations. The country is also reversing its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility.

title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
summary: The global nickel market faces a critical inflection point as seasonal production patterns in the Philippines and Indonesia's regulatory crackdowns remove significant supply, with 36 nickel producers removed from operations. Western Mines Group is also developing a new large ultramafic nickel deposit in Western Australia.

title: Can nickel prices hit $25,000 in 2026?
summary: Indonesia, producing two-thirds of the world's nickel, is delaying mining permits and slashing output quotas to support prices, revealing that much of the supposed surplus was a statistical illusion with only 55% capacity utilization in 2025. Analysts suggest prices could test $25,000 per tonne under a tightening scenario, though a clearing range of $20,000–$22,000 is more defensible.

title: Boom-Bust-Bounce: Understanding Nickel's Latest Price Cycle
summary: Global nickel prices retreated 2.45% on June 19, 2026, settling at $17,564.51 per ton, driven by Indonesia's decision to lower its benchmark nickel reference price signaling robust supply availability. Despite the drop, a realistic stable price range is likely to remain between $20,000 and $22,000 per tonne.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article examines how nickel production can be made more sustainable through better ESG practices, including stronger stakeholder engagement, transparency, and disclosure standards. It also highlights technologies and operational changes that can reduce greenhouse gas emissions, improve energy efficiency, and lower water use.

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials developed a carbon-free nickel extraction method using hydrogen plasma instead of carbon-based processes. The method is reported to cut CO2 emissions sharply, improve energy efficiency, and make low-grade nickel ores more usable for batteries and stainless steel.
